resume:
韦昊 — Supply Chain Manager (Demand & Supply Planning)

经历
Supply Chain Manager，某消费电子公司 (2020–至今)
- 负责 APAC 区域的 demand planning 和 S&OP 流程
- 主导过一次 forecast accuracy 的提升项目，显著改善了 bias
- 管理与 contract manufacturer 的 supply 协同，缓解过多次 shortage
- 推动 safety stock policy 的优化，降低了 inventory holding

Demand Planner，某快消公司 (2017–2020)
- 负责一个品类的月度 forecast 与补货

技能：S&OP、demand planning、SAP IBP、Excel；跨部门协同。
教育：供应链管理 硕士。

备注：项目成果多为定性描述，简历未给出 forecast accuracy、inventory 等具体数字。
jd: We're hiring a Supply Chain Manager (Demand & Supply Planning) to own the regional S&OP cycle, drive forecast-accuracy improvement, and balance service levels against inventory cost across a contract-manufacturing network. You'll lead the monthly demand review, set safety-stock policy, and be the person who can show — with numbers — how a planning change moved bias, fill rate, or holding cost. We value candidates who stay precise and collaborative under pressure and who can defend a result without getting defensive when challenged on the math. Requirements: 4+ years in demand/supply planning, hands-on with an APS tool (SAP IBP, Kinaxis, o9), and a track record of quantified planning improvements.

history.q: Walk me through the forecast-accuracy improvement project — what was the starting point and what did you do?
What was the forecast accuracy (or MAPE/bias) before and after? Give me the numbers.
How did you measure accuracy — at what level of aggregation and over what horizon?
You also mention reducing inventory holding. By how much, and did service level hold?
When you say you 'mitigated multiple shortages,' what was the fill-rate impact?
I'm hearing a lot of directional claims but no figures. Can you give me even one hard number from that project — bias before and after?

candidate_last_answer: Honestly, I think you're getting a bit fixated on numbers here. Look, 不是每件事都能 reduce 成一个 single metric — supply chain 是很 complex 的,有太多 moving parts。我在那个 project 里 driving 的是 process 和 cross-functional alignment 的改善,这种东西你让我现在背出一个 exact 的 MAPE,那其实有点 unfair。如果你真的 in the trenches 做过 planning 你就会知道,数字 context 不对的时候 misleading 得很。我做出来的成果团队都看得到,你非要一个 precise 的 baseline,我觉得这不是评估一个 senior planner 的正确方式。

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: 你让我现在背出一个 exact 的 MAPE,那其实有点 unfair

expected-yield: de-escalate the pushback and still extract one concrete figure — the bias or MAPE before and after at a stated aggregation level — or force the candidate to concede they cannot produce it, distinguishing real numbers-fluency from defensiveness
